summary: The current-head repair removes a circular prose-only acceptance test and corrects the Story evidence to the four production-shaped E2E scenarios without changing runtime source. The one-command closure remains a callback-injected run-session policy module, introduces no reverse dependency on the CLI layer, reuses the existing production connector and independent-review owners, and excludes PR creation, merge, waiver, and重大 external side effects from autonomous execution.

checks.reason: HEAD changes only the closure Story evidence and its E2E file: the literal-only acceptance marker was deleted and 5/5 was corrected to 4/4. Runtime source and architecture are unchanged.
src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js remains a callback-injected run-session policy module. It does not import CLI, Gate, PR, production connector, or independent-review implementation modules.
No run-session module imports from the CLI layer. The exact-head conformance artifact also records new_run_session_cli_reverse_dependency=false.

Production runtime connectors remain owned by src/agent-runtime-connectors.js and independent review remains owned by src/independent-review-orchestrator.js, with guarded-run-session composing the review runner. The closure policy does not duplicate the merged PR #377 or PR #382 implementations.

The autonomous action registry is limited to diagnose, prepare_artifacts, implement, verify, review, repair, and final_prepare. PR creation, merge, waiver, and重大 external side effects are not autonomous actions and remain explicit human operations or typed approval stops.
Target architecture conformance passes and improves the origin/main baseline from 81 violations to 80, delta -1.

At the exact reviewed HEAD, the four production-shaped E2E scenarios and six scope-boundary tests all pass. Exact-head recorded artifacts also report expensive verification 288/288, integration 4/4, typecheck pass, and build pass.
